City of
Morgan Hill

17555 Peak Avenue, Morgan Hill, CA 95037

FPPC
Aug 27 10 cg ht

CITY ATTORNEY
Respond to:
499 So. Sunnyvale Ave.
Sunnyvale, CA 94086
(408) 738-8848

August 23, 1990

Kathryn Donovon, Esq.
General Counsel Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re:    Request for Advisory Letter Pursuant to
       Government Code Section 83114(b)

Dear Ms. Donovon:

     This letter is written on behalf of Councilmember
Lorraine F. Barke requesting an advisory letter pursuant to
Section 83114(b) of the Government Code and Title 2, California
Code of Regulations, Section 18329. Councilmember Barke is a
member of the Morgan Hill City Council. She also sits as a
member of the Morgan Hill Redevelopment Agency Board. Her
mailing address is 14530 De Anza Court, Morgan Hill, California
95037.

     Councilmember Barke has expressly authorized me to make
this request on her behalf. The request relates to a pending
decision before the Morgan Hill City Council. I have previously
spoken with Mr. John Wallace of your staff about this matter.

     A potential conflict of interest arises concerning
Councilmember Barke's business property in relation to the
development of the Morgan Hill Business Park. Councilmember
Barke and her husband are the owners and operators of a nursery
business located on Monterey Road in the City of Morgan Hill.
The nursery property is located within 2,500 feet of the Morgan
Hill Business Park property. Both Councilmember Barke's property
and the Business Park property are located within the Ojo De Agua
Community Development Project Area adopted by the Morgan Hill
Redevelopment Agency.

[The image appears to be almost entirely blank or very faintly printed, with only barely visible traces of what might be a book or document icon in the upper right corner. The rest of the page is white/blank with no clearly legible text to transcribe.]

Page - 2 -
August 23, 1990
Kathryn Donovan, Esq.

Because of the size of the Morgan Hill Business Park
property, it is being developed in phases under an overall
planned unit development agreement originally entered into
between Parkland's predecessor in interest and the City in 1981,
prior to Councilmember Barke's election to the City Council. The
present decision involves development of a portion of the
property which is more than 2,500 feet from Councilmember Barke's
nursery for an Auto Mall consisting of new and used car sales
agencies and related service and repair facilities. A map is
attached showing the relationship of the property and the
proposed projects. The Council will be voting on October 17,
1990 regarding this matter.

I am requesting that you render an opinion as to
whether Councilmember Barke may participate in decisions
affecting the Parkland property. I would appreciate your review
both as to the issue of material effect and whether or not
Councilmember Barke is affected in a manner distinguishable from
the general public. Based upon information supplied by the
Morgan Hill Chamber of Commerce, there are some 405 business
properties within the City of Morgan Hill. The materiality issue
may be determinative on a number of decisions if the conclusion
is that the effect is not material, since Councilmember Barke's
property is not directly subject to those decisions but affected
only by its proximity to them.

Thank you for your assistance on this matter. It is
contemplated that this matter will be presented to the City
Council at its meeting of October 17, 1990. At that point, it
will be necessary for me to provide Councilmember Barke with
advice as to her participation. A response by that time would be
very much appreciated. Please let me know if any additional
information is required.

Very truly yours,

MARC G/ HYNES
City Attorney

Cochrane

FFPC

SEP 25 1 :50 P.I 90                           City of
                                              Morgan Hill                    CITY ATTORNEY
                                                                             Respond to:
                                                                             499 So. Sunnyvale Ave.
                                              17555 Peak Avenue, Morgan Hill, CA 95037
                                                                             Sunnyvale, CA 94086
                                              September 25, 1990
                                                                             (408) 738-8848


Joseph Garcia, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

     Re:  Letter No. 90-564

     Dear Mr. Garcia:

          I am writing this letter to you on the basis of the letter I
received from Mr. Scott Hallabrin, the Acting General Counsel, dated
September 7, 1990. I also write further to our telephone conversation
on September 24 regarding my request for legal advice concerning Council
Member Barke.

          My original letter had attached to it a map showing the relative
location of the lands of Parkland property and the commercial property
owned by Council Member Barke.  Attached to this letter is a colored
version which shows, in yellow, the boundaries of the Parkland holdings.
The section marked in red represents the portion of the property which is
the subject of discussions concerning a possible auto mall.

          My initial concern related to the proximity of the property as
shown by the yellow boundary to Council Member Barke's property let alone
the location of that part of the property which is planned as the auto
mall. At present, as I understand it from the Director of Community
Development, the proposed auto mall facility parcels have not been separately
created as the map would suggest.  They are all still an individual part
of the yellow bounded parcel.  Please let me know if I can provide any
additional information to you.

                                              Very truly yours,


                                              MARC G. HYNES

CITY,MORGAN HILL                TEL: 4087793117        May 10,90 16:00 No.008 P.19




Marie Calanders

City of Berkeley

Office of the City Attorney
Martin Luther King, Jr.
Civic Center Building
2180 Milvia Street
Berkeley, California 94704
Telecommunications Device for the Deaf (415) 644-6915

Telephone: (415) 644-6380
Telecopy: (415) 644-8641

August 23, 1990                                    VIA FACSIMILE


Jonathan Rothman
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Re:  Request for Advice

Dear Jonathan:

On August 13, 1990, I phoned the FPPC and requested advice on
whether the City of Berkeley would be violating Govt. Code
section 89001 if the Voter Information Pamphlet publishes with
the candidates' statements also contained their photographs. The
attorney of the day, John Wallace, advised me that the inclusion
of the photographs would probably be a violation of the
prohibition against mass mailings at public expense contained at
Government Code section 89001 because it would include photos of
elected officials seeking reelection. Mr. Wallace suggested
that the issue we raised would be better addressed by our
requesting a written advice letter. Please consider this a
request for written advice.

It is our belief that the inclusion of candidates' photos in the
Voter Information Pamphlet falls within the exception of 2
Cal.Admin. Code section 18901(b)(7) as it is required by law to
be sent to the registered voters. The Charter of the City of
Berkeley Section 6.5, provides that a candidate for local office
may file a statement of his/her qualifications for office and a
recent photograph with the City Clerk. If the candidate files a
photograph with his/her statement, the Charter requires that "The
City Clerk shall cause said candidate's statements to be printed
in some convenient form and shall mail a copy of said statements
to each registered voter with the sample ballot. . ." (Attached
is a copy of section 6.5.) The Charter does not give the City
Clerk discretion to omit the photograph if the candidate has
submitted it and it has always been the City's practice to
require the publication of the photograph.

Jonathan Rothman
California Fair Political Practices Commission
August 23, 1990
Page 2


Additionally, the FPPC's interpretation of the mass mailing
prohibition puts the City in the unusual position of having to
exclude from the pamphlet only those photos of candidates who are
currently elected officers, while the City would still be
required to publish the photographs of all others seeking
elective office.

We understand that the Commission can take up to twenty-one
working day's to respond to requests for advice. However,
because the City must submit the Voter Information Pamphlet to
the County Registrar of Voters for publication by August 31,
1990, we would appreciate your response by August 30th.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

MANUELA ALBUQUERQUE
City Attorney

SARAH REYNOSO
Deputy City Attorney

SR/lh

Enclosure

CHARTER OF THE CITY OF BERKELEY
Sections 5 to 6 1/2, Article III

(10) Canvass of returns and declaration of results.
The City Council shall meet at its usual place of meeting on
the first Tuesday after the election, including any run-off
election, to receive the certification of results prepared by the
City Clerk. The City Clerk shall canvass the results of the
election in accordance with procedures established in the State of
California Elections Code. The persons having the majority of
the votes given for Mayor, Auditor and for Councilmembers from
each Council District, and the persons having the highest number
of votes given for each other office, shall be declared elected.

(11) Use of voting machines, voting devices or vote
tabulating devices.
If the use of voting machines, voting devices or vote
tabulating devices at a municipal election is authorized by the
City Council, any of the provisions of this Section 5 may be
modified by the City Council to the extent necessary to permit the
use of such voting machines, voting devices or vote tabulating
devices.

(12) (Repealed)

Section 6. Votes for Deceased Candidates.
Whenever a candidate whose name appears upon the ballot at a
general municipal election dies before the time of closing
of the polls on the day of election, the votes cast for such
deceased candidate shall be counted in determining the results of
the election for the office for which the decedent was a
candidate. If the deceased candidate receives the highest number
of the votes cast for the office, or if more than one is to be
elected to such office and the deceased candidate receives
sufficient votes to be one of those elected for such office, he or
she shall be considered elected and the office to which he or she
was elected shall be vacant at the beginning of the term for which
he or she was elected. The vacancy thus created shall be filled
in the same manner as if the candidate had died subsequent to
taking office for that term.

Section 6 1/2. Candidate's Statement of Qualifications.
At the time of filing his or her nomination papers, each
candidate for an elective office with the City Clerk shall file a
verified statement showing the name of the candidate, the office
for which he or she is a candidate, his or her place of residence,
place of birth, present occupation, what public offices he or she
has held, whether he or she is a taxpayer in the City of Berkeley,
a statement giving information as to his or her experience and
qualifications, and a recent photograph, to the end that the
electors may be in a position to estimate his or her fitness to

3

CHARTER OF THE CITY OF BERKELEY
Section 6 1/2, Article III

fill the office, and the names of not less than five or more than
twenty residents of the City of Berkeley to whom he or she refers.
Until otherwise provided by ordinance, such statements shall not
exceed two hundred words in length. At the time of filing said
statement, each candidate shall also pay to the City Clerk a
printing fee which, until otherwise provided by ordinance, shall
be the sum of $35.00. The City Clerk shall cause said candidates'
statements to be printed in some convenient form and shall mail a
copy of said statements to each registered voter with the sample
ballot, provided that no name to which the candidate refers shall
be included in the publication by the clerk unless the written
consent of the person named is filed with the City Clerk. The
provisions of this section are self-executing, but the City
Council, by ordinance, may more definitely prescribe the form of
said candidate's statement. The printing fees so collected by
the City Clerk shall be paid into the City Treasury, and the
expense of printing said candidates' statements shall be paid from
the City Treasury. No refund from printing fees shall be made to
candidates, nor shall any extra charge be made, regardless of
whether the printing expense is more or less than the amount of
the fees received.

4
